title: Quobly And Entropica Labs Partner To Accelerate Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing
why:
- Quobly and Entropica Labs have signed a strategic Memorandum of Understanding to accelerate the development of fault-tolerant quantum computing technologies.
- The partnership focuses on software-hardware co-design, integrating Quobly’s silicon quantum processor architecture with Entropica Labs’ fault-tolerant quantum software stack from the earliest stages of system design.
- The collaboration aims to jointly develop advanced compiler and optimization technologies for fault-tolerant quantum error correction (FTQEC), tailored to Quobly’s silicon architecture.
- Quobly is leveraging €40 million in recent funding to expand its operations in Asia, particularly in Singapore, and to accelerate development of industrial-grade, error-corrected quantum computing systems.
- This partnership highlights a shift in the quantum sector from focusing solely on qubit physics to addressing system-level fault tolerance, which is essential for scalable, real-world quantum computing applications.
